elderlyWarning: This link contains an '@' sign which is used to hide the real website address. The part before the '@' is fake — do NOT click it.
This link goes to an IP address (like 192.168.1.1) instead of a real website name. Real banks and shops never do this.
This link pretends to be a bank or well-known company and asks you to login or verify. This is a common scam — do NOT enter your password.
This link says you won a prize or gift. This is almost always a scam to steal your personal information or money.
This link tries to scare you with urgent words like 'act now' or 'account blocked'. Scammers use fear to make you act quickly without thinking.
This link uses a lot of hidden codes to disguise its real destination. Legitimate websites do not need to hide their address.
This is a shortened link that hides the real website address. You cannot tell where it leads. Be very careful before clicking.
This link uses an unusual web address ending (.xyz, .top, .tk) that is commonly used for scam websites.
